unverified.official_structure: Hierarchical: 5 domains, each with 3 facets = 15 facets (Extraversion: Sociability/Assertiveness/Energy; Agreeableness: Compassion/Respectfulness/Trust; Conscientiousness: Organization/Productiveness/Responsibility; Negative Emotionality: Anxiety/Depression/Emotional Volatility; Open-Mindedness: Intellectual Curiosity/Aesthetic Sensitivity/Creative Imagination).
unverified.discrepancy: Title advertises the BFI-2's defining 'Facet' structure, but the test contains only the 5 domain-level dimensions with no facets. It is effectively a plain Big Five domain quiz mislabeled as a facet instrument; the hierarchical facet layer that distinguishes the BFI-2 is entirely missing.
unverified.proposed_fix: Either rename to a BFI-2 domain-level test, or add facet dimensions (at minimum one representative facet per domain) so the 'Facet' framing is honored.

unverified.framework: Dunn Sensory Profile (Model of Sensory Processing)
unverified.official_structure: Dunn's model = exactly 4 quadrants: Low Registration, Sensation Seeking, Sensory Sensitivity, Sensation Avoiding. No 5th 'Regulation' quadrant exists.
unverified.discrepancy: Test adds an invented 5th dimension 'Regulation' on top of Dunn's four correct quadrants, breaking the known 4-quadrant shape.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'regulation' dimension (and its 2 items); Dunn Sensory Profile = 4 quadrants (Registration, Seeking, Sensitivity, Avoiding).

unverified.framework: Gottman Sound Relationship House (SRH)
unverified.official_structure: SRH = 7 levels (Love Maps, Fondness & Admiration, Turn Toward, The Positive Perspective, Manage Conflict, Make Life Dreams Come True, Create Shared Meaning) + 2 weight-bearing walls (Trust, Commitment).
unverified.discrepancy: Test has 6 of 7 floors; the 'Positive Perspective' level is missing (Trust/Commitment walls also absent).
unverified.proposed_fix: Add a 'Positive Perspective' dimension (2 balanced items) to restore the SRH's 7-level shape.

unverified.framework: Adult attachment / relationship-repair (claims scientific-instrument tier)
unverified.official_structure: No validated public instrument named 'Relationship Attachment Repair' with factors Reassurance/Space/Accountability/Understanding/Ritual exists. Established attachment scales (ECR-R, RSQ, AAS) use anxiety/avoidance or self/other model dimensions, not these.
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A (published scientific instrument) but the 5 dimensions are a bespoke repair-needs taxonomy with no authoritative published factor structure to check against.
unverified.proposed_fix: Re-tier to B/generic (it is not a recognized validated instrument); do not present as an established scientific attachment measure.

unverified.framework: Hogan Personality Inventory (HPI)
unverified.official_structure: HPI = 7 primary scales: Adjustment, Ambition, Sociability, Interpersonal Sensitivity, Prudence, Inquisitive, Learning Approach.
unverified.discrepancy: Test has 6 of the 7 HPI scales; 'Interpersonal Sensitivity' is missing (scale names Ambition/Adjustment/Sociability/Prudence/Inquisitive/Learning are distinctly Hogan, so the omission is a shape gap).
unverified.proposed_fix: Add an 'Interpersonal Sensitivity' dimension (2 items) so the framework matches HPI's 7 scales.

unverified.framework: Entrepreneurial Orientation (Lumpkin & Dess; Miller/Covin-Slevin)
unverified.official_structure: EO = 5 dimensions (Lumpkin & Dess 1996): Innovativeness, Proactiveness, Risk-taking, Autonomy, Competitive Aggressiveness — or the 3-factor Miller/Covin-Slevin core (Innovativeness, Proactiveness, Risk-taking). 'Resilience' is not an EO dimension.
unverified.discrepancy: Test includes the 5 correct EO dimensions but adds an invented 6th factor 'Resilience' that is not part of the EO construct.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'resilience' dimension (and its 2 items); EO = 5 factors (Lumpkin & Dess).

unverified.framework: Contingencies of Self-Worth Scale (Crocker et al., 2003)
unverified.official_structure: 7 domains: Others' Approval, Appearance, Competition (outdoing others), Academic Competence, Family Support, Virtue, God's Love
unverified.discrepancy: Has 8 dims instead of 7. Drops the official 'Competition' domain and invents two non-CSW contingencies: 'autonomy' and 'love'. Only 6 of the 8 (approval, appearance, competence, virtue, family, faith=God's love) map to real CSW domains.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove 'autonomy' and 'love'; add 'competition' domain; official CSW = 7 domains (approval, appearance, competition, academic competence, family support, virtue, God's love).

unverified.framework: Adult Temperament Questionnaire (Evans & Rothbart, 2007)
unverified.official_structure: 4 higher-order factors: Effortful Control, Negative Affect, Extraversion/Surgency, Orienting Sensitivity
unverified.discrepancy: Has 5 top-level dims instead of the known 4. 'Activation' is invented as a 5th factor; in the real ATQ 'Activation Control' is a subscale within Effortful Control, not a separate temperament dimension.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'activation' dimension (fold its items into effortfulControl); ATQ = 4 factors: Effortful Control, Negative Affect, Extraversion/Surgency, Orienting Sensitivity.

unverified.framework: Buss-Perry Aggression Questionnaire (BPAQ)
unverified.official_structure: 4 subscales: Physical Aggression, Verbal Aggression, Anger, Hostility
unverified.discrepancy: Adds an invented 5th dimension 'Repair' (post-anger reparation); the BPAQ has no such factor. Framework shape is 4 factors, not 5.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'repair' dimension and its 2 items; keep only physical, verbal, anger, hostility (BPAQ = 4 factors).

unverified.framework: Interpersonal Reactivity Index (Davis IRI)
unverified.official_structure: 4 subscales: Perspective Taking, Empathic Concern, Fantasy, Personal Distress
unverified.discrepancy: Adds an invented 5th dimension 'Action' (practical helping/follow-through); the IRI has no behavioral/action subscale. Framework shape is 4 factors, not 5.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'action' dimension and its 2 items; keep only perspective, concern, fantasy, distress (IRI = 4 subscales).

unverified.framework: Cultural Intelligence Scale (CQS; Ang & Van Dyne four-factor model)
unverified.official_structure: 4 factors: Metacognitive (Strategy), Cognitive (Knowledge), Motivational (Drive), Behavioral CQ
unverified.discrepancy: Adds an invented 5th dimension 'Humility'; cultural humility is a separate construct, not part of the four-factor CQS. Framework shape is 4 factors, not 5.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'humility' dimension and its 2 items; keep drive, knowledge, strategy, behavior (CQS = 4 factors).

unverified.framework: Work Locus of Control Scale (Spector 1988)
unverified.official_structure: Unidimensional internal-external continuum (8 internal + 8 external items scored on one dimension); at most a 2-factor internal/external split in later CFA work
unverified.discrepancy: Splits a unidimensional scale into 6 invented facets (effort, skill, systems, leadership, luck, network). The WLCS has no such facet structure; internal vs external attribution is the only distinction.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to the internal-external structure: group effort+skill as 'Internal' and systems+leadership+luck+network as 'External' (one bipolar dimension, or 2 subscales), matching the WLCS.

unverified.framework: HIGH5 Strengths Test (HIGH5 Test)
unverified.official_structure: 4 domains: Doing, Thinking, Feeling, Motivating (20 strengths, 5 per domain)
unverified.discrepancy: Uses 5 domains (Thinking, Doing, Relating, Influencing, Energizing). HIGH5's actual domains are Doing, Thinking, Feeling, Motivating (only 2 overlap). 'Relating', 'Influencing', 'Energizing' are not HIGH5 domains — they resemble Gallup CliftonStrengths domains, not HIGH5.
unverified.proposed_fix: Replace dimensions with HIGH5's 4 domains: Doing, Thinking, Feeling, Motivating; drop the invented Relating/Influencing/Energizing.

unverified.framework: MBTI Step II (facet-level)
unverified.official_structure: 20 facets — 5 per dichotomy (e.g. E-I: Initiating-Receiving, Expressive-Contained, Gregarious-Intimate, Active-Reflective, Enthusiastic-Quiet)
unverified.discrepancy: Presents only the 4 top-level dichotomies as 8 poles (expressive/reflective=E-I, realistic/conceptual=S-N, logical/empathetic=T-F, planned/adaptive=J-P). This is the Step I structure; the defining feature of Step II is its 20 facets, which are entirely absent.
unverified.proposed_fix: Either rename to a plain MBTI (Step I) style test, or expand to the 20 Step II facets (5 per dichotomy) to match the claimed framework.

unverified.framework: Multidimensional Personality Questionnaire (MPQ, Tellegen)
unverified.official_structure: 3 higher-order factors: Positive Emotionality (PEM), Negative Emotionality (NEM), Constraint (CON) — sometimes a 4-factor solution adds Absorption (ABS)
unverified.discrepancy: positive/negative/constraint map correctly to PEM/NEM/CON, and absorption is a legitimate 4th factor/primary scale, but 'agency' is not an MPQ factor or primary scale — it is invented.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the invented 'agency' dimension; keep positive/negative/constraint (the 3 higher-order factors) plus optionally absorption as the recognized 4th factor.

unverified.framework: National Career Clusters Interest Survey (US Dept of Education / Advance CTE)
unverified.official_structure: 16 Career Clusters (14 in the revised Advance CTE framework); the survey scores across all clusters, not a small set of interest buckets
unverified.discrepancy: Test uses 7 invented broad interest areas (building/business/creative/helping/technical/public/science) that do not correspond to the 16 named Career Clusters. This is a wrong dimension set, not merely fewer items.
unverified.proposed_fix: Rename dimensions to (a representative subset of) the 16 National Career Clusters, or relabel the test as a generic 'career interest areas' quiz rather than the Career Clusters framework.

unverified.framework: Strong Interest Inventory - Basic Interest Scales (BIS)
unverified.official_structure: 30 Basic Interest Scales nested within 6 General Occupational Themes (Holland RIASEC: Realistic, Investigative, Artistic, Social, Enterprising, Conventional)
unverified.discrepancy: Test uses 8 generic Prediger-style areas (people/things/data/ideas/business/arts/nature/service) that match neither the 30 BIS nor the 6 RIASEC themes the BIS are organized under.
unverified.proposed_fix: Restructure to the 6 RIASEC General Occupational Themes (or a subset of the 30 named BIS), or rename away from 'Basic Interest Scales'.

unverified.framework: Psychological Capital Questionnaire (PCQ; Luthans & Youssef-Morgan)
unverified.official_structure: 4 components (HERO): Hope, Efficacy, Resilience, Optimism
unverified.discrepancy: Test adds an invented 5th dimension 'Support', which is not part of the PsyCap HERO construct.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'support' dimension and its 2 items; official PsyCap = 4 factors (Hope, Efficacy, Resilience, Optimism).

unverified.framework: Coping Flexibility Scale (CFS/CFS-R; Kato, 2012)
unverified.official_structure: 2 factors: Evaluation Coping and Adaptive Coping (dual-process model of coping flexibility)
unverified.discrepancy: Test uses 5 general coping-style dims (problem/emotion/meaning/social/switching) drawn from Lazarus & Folkman coping types, not the 2-factor coping-flexibility construct. Only 'switching' relates to flexibility; the rest measure coping styles, a different construct.
unverified.proposed_fix: Restructure to 2 factors matching the CFS (Evaluation Coping = monitor/appraise effectiveness; Adaptive Coping = abandon and switch strategies), or rename to a general 'coping styles' test.

unverified.framework: Communication Patterns Questionnaire (Christensen & Sullaway) blended with Gottman constructs
unverified.official_structure: The CPQ measures 3 patterns: mutual constructive communication, demand/withdraw, and mutual avoidance/withholding. No single published instrument has this test's exact 6 factors.
unverified.discrepancy: The 6 dims are a composite: pursuit/withdrawal = CPQ demand-withdraw; criticism/defensiveness = Gottman Four Horsemen; repair = Gottman repair attempts; solving = constructive communication. Each construct is individually validated, but they do not map to one published factor structure, so exact-match fidelity cannot be verified.

unverified.framework: General relationship-boundaries construct (no single validated instrument)
unverified.official_structure: No authoritative published factor structure exists for a validated 'relationship boundaries' instrument; the 6 dims (closeness/time/emotional-labor/privacy/conflict/reciprocity) are a reasonable but non-canonical taxonomy.
unverified.discrepancy: No published psychometric instrument with a defined factor structure to check against.

unverified.framework: Autism Spectrum Quotient (AQ, Baron-Cohen 2001)
unverified.official_structure: 5 subscales: Social Skill, Communication, Attention to Detail, Attention Switching, Imagination
unverified.discrepancy: Count is right (5) but two subscales are wrong: Communication is dropped, and 'Sensory' is invented (sensory sensitivity is not an AQ subscale). 'Routine' is a loose rename of Attention Switching.
unverified.proposed_fix: Replace 'Sensory' with 'Communication' and rename 'Routine' to 'Attention Switching' so the five dims map to the AQ subscales (Social, Communication, Details, Attention Switching, Imagination).

unverified.framework: Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale (ASRS v1.1, WHO)
unverified.official_structure: 2 subscales: Inattention and Hyperactivity/Impulsivity (18 DSM-based items)
unverified.discrepancy: Uses 6 invented dimensions (Focus, Organization, Time, Restlessness, Emotion, Follow-Through) instead of the ASRS's two factors; 'Emotion' is not an ASRS domain at all. Shape does not match the instrument the title names.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to the ASRS 2-factor shape: Inattention (focus, organization, time, follow-through) and Hyperactivity/Impulsivity (restlessness); drop or fold in 'Emotion' which is outside ASRS.

unverified.framework: CBT cognitive distortions (Beck / Burns)
unverified.official_structure: A clinical catalogue of ~10-15 distortions, not a validated confirmatory factor model; the 6 chosen (Catastrophizing, Mind Reading, All-or-Nothing, Personalization, Shoulds, Discounting the Positive) are all genuine Burns distortions
unverified.discrepancy: No authoritative fixed factor count exists for cognitive distortions, so 'exact factor structure' cannot be verified. The 6 sampled distortions are all legitimate and faithfully worded.

unverified.framework: ADEPT-15 (Aon/cut-e adaptive personality assessment)
unverified.official_structure: 15 personality aspects grouped into 6 workstyles (Task, Adaptation, Achievement, Interaction, Emotional, Teamwork). The instrument is defined by its 15 traits / 6 styles.
unverified.discrepancy: Uses 7 arbitrary dimensions (adaptability, ambition, collaboration, composure, conceptual, diligence, humility). This matches neither the 15 aspects nor the 6 workstyles. Some names (Conceptual, Composure, Humility, Ambition) are real ADEPT aspects but the set is an ad-hoc subset, not the framework's shape.
unverified.proposed_fix: Model the known shape: either the 6 workstyles or a representative subset of the 15 named aspects. A 7-trait set matches neither published structure.

unverified.framework: Jackson Personality Inventory-Revised (JPI-R)
unverified.official_structure: 15 content scales grouped into 5 clusters (Analytical, Emotional, Extroverted, Opportunistic, Dependable). JPI deliberately does NOT use Big Five labels.
unverified.discrepancy: Dimensions are a Big Five mashup (openness, neuroticism, extraversion) plus trustworthiness, organization, risk, innovation. Openness/Neuroticism/Extraversion are not JPI clusters or scales; JPI's clusters are Analytical/Emotional/Extroverted/Opportunistic/Dependable. Only risk, organization, innovation loosely echo real JPI scales.
unverified.proposed_fix: Relabel to the 5 JPI clusters (Analytical, Extroverted, Emotional, Opportunistic, Dependable) or a subset of the 15 named scales; drop the Big Five factor names.

unverified.framework: Rosenberg Self-Esteem Scale (RSES)
unverified.official_structure: Unidimensional: a single global self-esteem factor scored across all 10 items (at most positive/negative-worded method factors in bifactor models).
unverified.discrepancy: Splits a unidimensional scale into 5 invented factors (Self-Respect, Acceptance, Competence, Comparison, Criticism). None of these are RSES subscales; RSES yields one global score.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to a single 'Global Self-Esteem' dimension (RSES is unidimensional); do not fabricate factor subscales.

unverified.framework: Coopersmith Self-Esteem Inventory (CSEI)
unverified.official_structure: 4 substantive subscales — General Self, Social Self-Peers, Home-Parents, School-Academic — plus a Lie (validity) scale excluded from the total.
unverified.discrepancy: Has 5 dimensions: personal, social, family, achievement map reasonably to General/Social-Peers/Home-Parents/School-Academic, but 'selfTrust' is an invented 5th subscale with no CSEI counterpart.
unverified.proposed_fix: Drop the invented 'Self-Trust' dimension; official CSEI = 4 subscales (Personal/General, Social-Peers, Home/Family, School/Achievement).

unverified.framework: Snyder Adult Hope Scale (AHS / Trait Hope Scale)
unverified.official_structure: 2 components — Agency (goal-directed motivation) and Pathways (perceived routes to goals) — measured by 4 items each, plus 4 filler items.
unverified.discrepancy: Has 5 dimensions: agency and pathways are correct, but obstacles, future, and support are invented and are not part of Snyder's hope theory/scale.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reduce to the 2 canonical dimensions (Agency, Pathways); remove the invented obstacles/future/support factors.

unverified.framework: Intolerance of Uncertainty Scale, short form (IUS-12)
unverified.official_structure: 2 factors — Prospective Anxiety (7 items) and Inhibitory Anxiety (5 items) — Carleton, Norton & Asmundson (2007).
unverified.discrepancy: Uses 6 invented dimensions (worry, planning, avoidance, reassurance, decision, flexibility). None correspond to the validated 2-factor IUS-12 structure; 'flexibility' items are even reverse-valenced content not in the construct.
unverified.proposed_fix: Restructure to 2 factors — Prospective Anxiety and Inhibitory Anxiety; fold the six ad-hoc dims into these two.

unverified.framework: Need for Affect Questionnaire (NAQ; Maio & Esses, 2001)
unverified.official_structure: 2 dimensions — Approach (motivation to approach emotions) and Avoidance (motivation to avoid emotions).
unverified.discrepancy: Has 5 dimensions: approach and avoidance are correct, but intensity, expression, and meaning are invented and not part of the NAQ two-factor structure.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reduce to the 2 NAQ dimensions (Approach, Avoidance); remove the invented intensity/expression/meaning factors.

unverified.framework: Regret proneness (no standard multidimensional instrument; cf. Schwartz et al. 2002 Regret Scale)
unverified.official_structure: No authoritative multidimensional 'regret proneness' instrument exists. The established Regret Scale (Schwartz et al., 2002) is 5-item unidimensional; the literature distinguishes action vs inaction regret conceptually (Gilovich & Medvec) but not as a validated 6-factor proneness measure.
unverified.discrepancy: Proposes a 6-factor structure (anticipated, rumination, replay, action, inaction, repair) that has no validated published factor model to check against. The action/inaction split is theory-grounded, but 'anticipated/rumination/replay/repair' as separate proneness factors are not established.
unverified.proposed_fix: No validated structure to match; either treat regret proneness as unidimensional (Schwartz Regret Scale) or, if multidimensional, ground it explicitly in the action-vs-inaction regret distinction rather than 6 ad-hoc factors.

unverified.framework: Self-disclosure (construct; no single proprietary instrument — cf. Jourard Self-Disclosure Questionnaire, Social Penetration Theory breadth/depth)
unverified.official_structure: No canonical 'Self-Disclosure Style' instrument. Research operationalizes disclosure via breadth/depth (Altman & Taylor Social Penetration Theory) and Jourard's Self-Disclosure Questionnaire (target-person x topic), not a fixed 6-factor style model.
unverified.discrepancy: The 6 dimensions (openness, pacing, depth, selectivity, reciprocity, privacy) are a plausible but non-authoritative taxonomy; depth and reciprocity echo real constructs. No published factor structure exists to grade against. '-style' framing is honest.

unverified.framework: Personal Values Card Sort (W.R. Miller, J. C'de Baca, D.B. Matthews, P.L. Wilbourne, 2001; Motivational Interviewing)
unverified.official_structure: The Personal Values Card Sort is a deck of ~83-100 value cards sorted by personal importance (Most/Very/Somewhat/Not Important). It is a clinical elicitation exercise with NO published factor structure or fixed set of scored value dimensions.
unverified.discrepancy: Assigned Tier A but the real tool has no factor structure to validate against; the 8 value dimensions (autonomy, connection, mastery, contribution, security, adventure, integrity, creativity) are an invented generic taxonomy, not the PVCS. Tier likely miscategorized (should be C or reframed as generic values).
unverified.proposed_fix: Reframe as a generic 'personal values' quiz (Tier C) rather than implying the specific Personal Values Card Sort instrument; or map dimensions to a real values model with a published structure (e.g. Schwartz's 10 values).

unverified.framework: Guilford-Zimmerman Temperament Survey (GZTS, 1949)
unverified.official_structure: 10 traits: General Activity, Restraint, Ascendance, Sociability, Emotional Stability, Objectivity, Friendliness, Thoughtfulness, Personal Relations, Masculinity (M).
unverified.discrepancy: 9 traits map correctly, but the 10th dimension 'Expression' (adapting self-presentation to context) substitutes for the GZTS trait Masculinity (M). 'Expression' is not a GZTS trait and its item content does not measure Masculinity-Femininity.
unverified.proposed_fix: Rename 'expression' to Masculinity-Femininity (M) and rewrite its two items to tough-minded-vs-sensitive content; official GZTS = 10 traits ending in Masculinity.

unverified.framework: Comrey Personality Scales (CPS; A.L. Comrey)
unverified.official_structure: 8 factors: Trust vs Defensiveness (T), Orderliness (O), Social Conformity (C), Activity (A), Emotional Stability (S), Extraversion (E), Masculinity vs Femininity / mental-toughness-vs-sensitivity (M), Empathy vs Egocentrism (P).
unverified.discrepancy: 7 CPS factors present, but 'Expression' (adapting roles/self-presentation) substitutes for the CPS factor Masculinity vs Femininity (M). Expression is not a CPS scale and its content mismatches M (tough-minded vs sensitive).
unverified.proposed_fix: Replace 'expression' with Masculinity-Femininity (M) and rewrite its two items to tough-minded-vs-sensitive content.

unverified.framework: Six Factor Personality Questionnaire (SFPQ; Jackson, Paunonen, Tremblay; SIGMA Assessment Systems)
unverified.official_structure: 6 factors (18 facets): Industriousness, Methodicalness, Independence, Extraversion, Agreeableness, Openness to Experience. (Splits Conscientiousness into Industriousness + Methodicalness; omits Neuroticism, adds Independence.)
unverified.discrepancy: Only 3 factors correct (Industriousness, Independence, Methodicalness). 'Enthusiasm', 'Assertiveness', and 'Sensitivity' are invented and do not correspond to the SFPQ factors Extraversion, Agreeableness, and Openness to Experience; Openness to Experience is entirely missing.
unverified.proposed_fix: Rename the three non-matching dims to Extraversion, Agreeableness, and Openness to Experience and rewrite their item pairs to match SFPQ content.

unverified.framework: Career Adapt-Abilities Scale (CAAS), Savickas & Porfeli 2012
unverified.official_structure: 4 factors ('4 Cs'): Concern, Control, Curiosity, Confidence — 6 items each, 24 total. No 'Support' or 'Experimentation' factor.
unverified.discrepancy: Correctly includes the 4 Cs but adds two invented, non-CAAS factors: 'Support' and 'Experimentation'. Tier A requires the published factor structure exactly.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'support' and 'experimentation' dimensions and their 4 items; keep only the 4 Cs (Concern, Control, Curiosity, Confidence). Official CAAS = 4 factors.

unverified.framework: Affective Neuroscience Personality Scales (ANPS), Davis & Panksepp
unverified.official_structure: ANPS measures 6 primary emotional systems: SEEKING, CARE, PLAY, FEAR, ANGER, SADNESS, plus an optional Spirituality scale. It does not include a 'Regulation' scale — it assesses raw affective dispositions, not emotion regulation.
unverified.discrepancy: The 6 Panksepp systems are correct, but the test adds a 7th non-ANPS dimension, 'Regulation', which is not part of the instrument and conceptually contradicts it (ANPS measures unregulated primary affect).
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'regulation' dimension and its 2 items; keep only the 6 ANPS emotional systems (or, if desired, add the optional 'Spirituality' scale instead).

unverified.framework: Kuder Preference Record / Kuder Career Interests
unverified.official_structure: 10 canonical interest scales: Outdoor, Mechanical, Computational, Scientific, Persuasive, Artistic, Literary, Musical, Social Service, Clerical.
unverified.discrepancy: The test reproduces 9 of the 10 canonical Kuder scales but omits the Musical scale, dropping one member of a well-defined, closed 10-scale set the test is clearly attempting to mirror.
unverified.proposed_fix: Add a 'musical' dimension (2 items, e.g. interest in playing/creating/attending music) to complete the canonical Kuder 10-scale structure.

unverified.framework: Campbell Interest and Skill Survey (CISS)
unverified.official_structure: 7 Orientation Scales: Influencing, Organizing, Helping, Creating, Analyzing, Producing, Adventuring.
unverified.discrepancy: The test uses the 6 Holland-aligned orientations but omits Adventuring, the seventh CISS Orientation and the one scale that distinguishes the CISS from a plain RIASEC/six-theme model.
unverified.proposed_fix: Add an 'adventuring' dimension (2 items, e.g. interest in physical daring, athletics, military/adventure activities) to restore the full 7-orientation CISS shape.

unverified.framework: "Social battery" — pop-psychology concept for social energy/introversion-extraversion
unverified.official_structure: None. "Social battery" is a colloquial pop concept with no validated instrument or published factor structure; the 4 dims (recharge, spark, selective, recovery) are invented.
unverified.discrepancy: Mis-tiered as A (public scientific instrument) but no authoritative scientific structure exists; dims are original constructs, not a published scale.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier C (entertainment) and ensure "for fun" framing; no scientific factor structure to conform to.

unverified.framework: "Emotional needs" — no single canonical instrument (competing models: Harley's 10 needs, Glasser, SDT, etc.)
unverified.official_structure: No agreed authoritative factor structure. Multiple unrelated frameworks use the label; the 4 dims (security, connection, autonomy, recognition) do not map to any one validated scale.
unverified.discrepancy: Mis-tiered as A; there is no single published emotional-needs instrument to validate the dimension count/names against.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier C, or explicitly frame as an eclectic self-reflection tool rather than a scientific instrument.

unverified.framework: "Self-sabotage" — pop-psychology label, not a validated psychometric instrument
unverified.official_structure: No established diagnostic construct or published factor structure for "self-sabotage." The 4 dims (avoidance, perfectionism, visibility, conflict) are original groupings.
unverified.discrepancy: Mis-tiered as A; no authoritative scientific structure exists to check against.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier C and use reflective (non-diagnostic) framing.

unverified.framework: Codependency (Spann-Fischer Codependency Scale / Holyoake Codependency Index)
unverified.official_structure: Contested construct: Wikipedia notes codependency "has no established definition or diagnostic criteria within the mental health community." Existing scales disagree (Spann-Fischer is unidimensional; HCI reports 3 factors). The 4 dims here (caretaking, boundaries, approval, responsibility) match no single validated structure.
unverified.discrepancy: Mis-tiered as A; no consensus authoritative factor structure to validate against.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier C or frame as informal self-reflection; if kept, do not present as a validated diagnostic scale.

unverified.framework: Loner vs socializer — pop framing of introversion-extraversion / sociability
unverified.official_structure: No authoritative "loner vs socializer" instrument. The underlying trait (extraversion/sociability) is a Big Five facet, but the 4 dims (loner, socializer, one-on-one, balanced) are ad-hoc types, not a published factor structure.
unverified.discrepancy: Mis-tiered as A; the four categories are invented profile buckets rather than validated factors.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier C, or reframe as a facet of Big Five extraversion rather than a standalone scientific test.

unverified.framework: Assertiveness / assertive communication
unverified.official_structure: Established assertiveness instruments (e.g. Rathus Assertiveness Schedule, Gambrill & Richey Assertion Inventory) are scored as essentially unidimensional total-assertiveness scales; there is no authoritative public 'assertive communication' instrument with distinct published subscales named clarity / respect / boundaries / feedback.
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A (public scientific instrument) with a 4-factor structure, but the four dimensions are rationally invented, not derived from any published factor analysis. No canonical multi-factor 'Assertive Communication Test' exists to validate against.
unverified.proposed_fix: Treat assertiveness as a single unidimensional scale, or reclassify this quiz to Tier B/C as an inspired-by composite; do not present the 4 invented facets as an established scientific factor structure.

unverified.framework: Readiness for change (Transtheoretical Model / URICA / ADKAR)
unverified.official_structure: The dominant scientific change-readiness models use stages/constructs such as TTM stages (Precontemplation, Contemplation, Preparation, Action, Maintenance) with Decisional Balance, Self-Efficacy, and Processes of Change; ADKAR uses Awareness, Desire, Knowledge, Ability, Reinforcement. None use curiosity / stability / action / support.
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A but the four dimensions (curiosity, stability, action, support) map to no published change-readiness factor structure; they are ad hoc.
unverified.proposed_fix: Align dimensions to a documented model (e.g. TTM/URICA stages or ADKAR components) or reclassify to Tier B/C as a non-scientific composite; the current 4 dims have no published basis.

unverified.framework: Chinese zodiac (Sheng Xiao)
unverified.official_structure: The Chinese zodiac is known for 12 animal signs (Rat, Ox, Tiger, Rabbit, Dragon, Snake, Horse, Goat, Monkey, Rooster, Dog, Pig); it is secondarily associated with the five Wu Xing elements (Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal, Water). There is no standard grouping into 5 personality archetypes.
unverified.discrepancy: Uses 5 invented archetypes (initiator, strategist, nurturer, performer, guardian) that correspond to neither the 12 animal signs nor the 5 Wu Xing elements, so the recognizable shape of the framework is not reflected.
unverified.proposed_fix: Map dimensions to the recognizable Chinese-zodiac shape — either the 12 animal signs or the 5 Wu Xing elements (Wood/Fire/Earth/Metal/Water) — instead of 5 generic archetypes.

unverified.framework: Numerology (Pythagorean life path / personality numbers)
unverified.official_structure: Core numerology reduces to the single digits 1-9 (nine life-path/personality numbers), plus master numbers 11, 22, 33. The nine archetypes are roughly: 1 Leader, 2 Harmonizer/Diplomat, 3 Creative/Communicator, 4 Builder, 5 Explorer/Freedom, 6 Nurturer/Caregiver, 7 Seeker/Analyst, 8 Achiever/Power, 9 Humanitarian.
unverified.discrepancy: Uses only 6 archetypes (Leader, Harmonizer, Creator, Builder, Explorer, Seeker), mapping to numbers 1,2,3,4,5,7. The three remaining core life-path numbers are missing: 6 (Nurturer/Caregiver), 8 (Achiever/Power), 9 (Humanitarian). The framework's known shape is 9 numbers, not 6.
unverified.proposed_fix: Add three dimensions to cover all 9 life-path numbers: 'nurturer' (6, caregiving/responsibility), 'achiever' (8, power/material mastery), 'humanitarian' (9, compassion/idealism), each with 2 balanced items.

unverified.framework: Bespoke 'love communication' quiz loosely themed on Gary Chapman's 5 Love Languages, but not a real proprietary instrument
unverified.official_structure: No proprietary instrument named 'Communication Love Style' exists. The nearest real framework, Chapman's 5 Love Languages, has 5 fixed categories (Words of Affirmation, Quality Time, Acts of Service, Receiving Gifts, Physical Touch). This test instead uses 6 invented dimensions (reassurance, curiosity, planning, play, repair, presence) and does not claim to be Love Languages.
unverified.discrepancy: Dimensions do not map to any published instrument; they are original constructs. Not a structural error since the test does not present itself as the 5 Love Languages, but there is no authoritative shape to validate against.
unverified.proposed_fix: No structural fix required. Ensure intro frames it as an original 'inspired-by' communication-style reflection, not as the 5 Love Languages or any official instrument.

unverified.framework: Bespoke 'breakup coping style' quiz; no established proprietary instrument of this name
unverified.official_structure: No authoritative or proprietary 'Breakup Style' instrument exists. The 5 invented dimensions (closure, clean break, reflective, support, restart) are a reasonable original taxonomy of post-breakup coping, each with 2 balanced items, but there is no published structure to validate against.
unverified.discrepancy: No external framework to confirm dimension count/names; construction is internally coherent.
unverified.proposed_fix: No structural fix required. Ensure intro frames it as an original reflective quiz rather than a validated psychological instrument.

unverified.framework: Romantic jealousy; closest validated instrument = Multidimensional Jealousy Scale (Pfeiffer & Wong, 1989)
unverified.official_structure: The validated Multidimensional Jealousy Scale has 3 factors: Cognitive, Emotional, and Behavioral jealousy. There is no published factor structure for a 5-factor 'jealousy trigger' model (attention/comparison/uncertainty/exclusion/past wounds).
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A (scientific instrument) but the 5 trigger-based dimensions are a bespoke construct that matches no validated jealousy instrument; the canonical MDJS uses a completely different 3-factor (cog/emo/beh) structure. Items are all forward-keyed (no reverse items), but for an invented construct that is not by itself disqualifying.
unverified.proposed_fix: Re-tier to C (entertainment) since no validated instrument matches this operationalization; OR if kept as a serious measure, restructure to the MDJS 3-factor model (cognitive/emotional/behavioral jealousy).

unverified.framework: Interpersonal boundaries in friendship — no validated multi-factor instrument exists for this construct
unverified.official_structure: There is no published, validated factor structure for 'friendship boundaries.' The 5 domains (time/emotion/money/access/loyalty) are a reasonable but bespoke taxonomy with no authoritative scientific source.
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A (scientific instrument) but no authoritative public structure exists to verify against; the dimensions are invented rather than drawn from a validated boundaries scale. All items forward-keyed.
unverified.proposed_fix: Re-tier to C (entertainment); no validated 'friendship boundaries' instrument exists to hold it to a Tier A bar.

unverified.framework: Emotional/interpersonal dependency; closest validated instruments = Interpersonal Dependency Inventory (Hirschfeld et al., 1977) and Spouse-Specific Dependency Scale
unverified.official_structure: The validated Interpersonal Dependency Inventory has 3 factors: Emotional Reliance on Another Person, Lack of Social Self-Confidence, and Assertion of Autonomy. No validated instrument uses the 4-factor reassurance/soothing/decisions/separation structure; it also overlaps heavily with ECR attachment-anxiety.
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A but the 4 dimensions match no validated dependency instrument (IDI = 3 different factors); this is a bespoke operationalization. All items forward-keyed.
unverified.proposed_fix: Re-tier to C (entertainment); OR to be a serious measure, align to a validated dependency model (e.g., IDI 3-factor) or to ECR attachment-anxiety.

unverified.framework: Rejection/abandonment sensitivity; validated instrument = Rejection Sensitivity Questionnaire / Adult-RSQ (Downey & Feldman 1996; Berenson et al. 2009)
unverified.official_structure: The A-RSQ operationalizes rejection sensitivity as anxious/angry expectancy x perceived likelihood across social situations; recent validation confirms a 2-factor structure (Concern vs. Expectancy). It is scenario-based, not a 4-factor trigger model (distance/inconsistency/conflict/replacement).
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A but the 4 trigger-based dimensions match no validated rejection/abandonment-sensitivity instrument; the RSQ/A-RSQ uses a fundamentally different expectancy x reaction structure. All items forward-keyed.
unverified.proposed_fix: Re-tier to C (entertainment); OR to be a serious measure, restructure toward the RSQ/A-RSQ expectancy-based model or ECR attachment-anxiety.

unverified.framework: Claims Tier A scientific status for 'forgiveness readiness', but no validated public instrument uses this factor structure
unverified.official_structure: Real validated forgiveness instruments have different structures: TRIM (Avoidance, Revenge, Benevolence), Heartland Forgiveness Scale (Self, Others, Situations), Enright Forgiveness Inventory. None define a 6-factor 'readiness' model (accountability, safety, time, empathy, boundaries, meaning). No authoritative public structure matches these dims.
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A (public scientific instrument) but 'forgiveness readiness' with these 6 factors is not a validated published construct; it is an original pop-psych taxonomy mis-classified as a scientific instrument.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier B/C (entertainment/approximation). If Tier A is intended, rebuild against a real instrument, e.g. TRIM's 3 factors (Avoidance, Revenge, Benevolence) or Heartland's 3 (Self, Others, Situations).

unverified.framework: Claims Tier A scientific status for 'trust issues', but no validated public trust instrument uses this factor structure
unverified.official_structure: Validated interpersonal-trust scales use different structures: Rempel-Holmes-Zanna Trust Scale (Predictability, Dependability, Faith) and Rotter Interpersonal Trust Scale (unidimensional). None match the 5 dims here (suspicion, verification, vulnerability, consistency, repair). No authoritative public structure corresponds.
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A but the 5-dimension 'trust issues' structure is an original construct, not a published/validated instrument. Mis-classified as scientific.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier B/C. If a scientific basis is wanted, align to a validated trust scale, e.g. Rempel et al. 3 factors (Predictability, Dependability, Faith).

unverified.framework: Claims Tier A scientific status for 'sales personality', but no validated public instrument uses this factor structure
unverified.official_structure: No validated public 'sales personality' factor model exists with these dims. The nearest known frameworks are commercial: The Challenger Sale (Dixon & Adamson) has 5 rep profiles (Challenger, Hard Worker, Relationship Builder, Lone Wolf, Reactive Problem Solver) — a different taxonomy. The 6 dims here (consultative, persuasive, relational, analytical, challenger, service) are sales-approach styles, not a validated personality instrument.
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier A (public scientific instrument) but 'sales personality' as structured here has no authoritative published factor structure; it is an original/commercial-flavored taxonomy mis-classified as scientific.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier B (proprietary '-style' approximation) and ensure framing reads as inspired-by, not a validated psychometric test.

unverified.framework: Customer service / support agent style (no single canonical proprietary instrument)
unverified.official_structure: No established, named proprietary instrument defines a fixed dimension set for 'customer service style.' Various vendor 'support persona' models exist but none is authoritative.
unverified.discrepancy: Tagged Tier B (proprietary -style approximation) but does not correspond to any named copyrighted instrument. The 6 dimensions (empathic, solution, technical, calming, policy, recovery) are an internally coherent original taxonomy, 2 balanced items each. No framing text in the batch claims official status, so no framing violation is visible.
unverified.proposed_fix: No structural fix needed. Optionally reclassify as an original/entertainment (Tier C) career-style quiz since no proprietary framework is being approximated; ensure intro reads as 'inspired-by/original,' not an official instrument.

unverified.framework: Founder / startup archetypes (no validated psychometric instrument)
unverified.official_structure: There is no published, peer-reviewed personality instrument with a validated factor structure for 'founder personality.' The 6 labels (visionary, operator, product, seller, community, researcher) map to informal startup-role archetypes popularized in VC/accelerator content, not to validated factors.
unverified.discrepancy: Mis-tiered: flagged Tier A (public scientific instrument requiring an exact published factor structure) but no such authoritative structure exists. The dimensions are invented role archetypes; treating them as scientific factors is unsupported. Item balance itself is fine (2 items/dim).
unverified.proposed_fix: Re-tier to C (entertainment archetype quiz); do not present as a scientific instrument. No factor-structure change required once re-tiered — just ensure framing is 'for fun,' not diagnostic/validated.

unverified.framework: None — labeled Tier A (scientific) but the 6 'pressure response' types (accelerator/freezer/planner/helper/challenger/stabilizer) do not correspond to any published, validated stress-response instrument
unverified.official_structure: No authoritative public scientific instrument named 'Pressure Personality Test' with these 6 factors exists. Stress-response science (e.g. fight/flight/freeze/fawn) does not map onto these 6 named archetypes as a validated factor structure.
unverified.discrepancy: Marked Tier A (public scientific instrument) but is an invented archetype framework with no published factor structure to validate against; keying is all positive with no reverse items.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier C (entertainment) OR B (inspired-by) since no validated scientific 'pressure personality' factor model exists; ensure framing does not imply a validated diagnostic.

unverified.framework: Emotional Labor (Hochschild / Grandey)
unverified.official_structure: The construct is known for 2 core strategies: Surface Acting and Deep Acting (some scales add a third genuine/automatic emotion component).
unverified.discrepancy: Keeps the correct Surface and Deep acting factors but inflates the shape with three invented dimensions (Boundary Holding, Caretaking, Authentic) that are not recognized emotional-labor strategies.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reduce to the 2 canonical factors (Surface Acting, Deep Acting); optionally keep a third 'Genuine/Authentic emotion' facet. Drop Boundary Holding and Caretaking as they belong to other constructs.

unverified.framework: Workplace motivation needs (blend of Self-Determination Theory / Pink's Drive)
unverified.official_structure: No single authoritative public instrument has exactly these 6 factors. SDT = 3 needs (Autonomy, Competence, Relatedness); Pink's Drive = 3 (Autonomy, Mastery, Purpose). This test is an eclectic composite (Autonomy, Mastery, Purpose, Recognition, Security, Connection).
unverified.discrepancy: Dimensions are a reasonable composite of motivation theories but do not map to one published factor structure, so exact-match Tier A validation is not possible.
unverified.proposed_fix: Either reframe as a generic 'motivation drivers' inventory (no single-source claim) or align to a named model, e.g. SDT's 3 needs (Autonomy, Competence/Mastery, Relatedness/Connection).

unverified.framework: Team Psychological Safety (Edmondson, 1999)
unverified.official_structure: Edmondson's construct is measured as a single (unidimensional) team-level 7-item scale, not multiple named sub-factors.
unverified.discrepancy: Splits a unidimensional construct into 5 invented facets (Speaking Up, Inviting Input, Uncertainty, Respect, Learning). These are related behaviors but not validated sub-dimensions of the scale.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to a single Psychological Safety scale (Edmondson is unidimensional); if facets are retained, frame them as illustrative behaviors, not distinct factors.

unverified.framework: Psychological Hardiness (Kobasa/Maddi)
unverified.official_structure: 3 dispositions ('three C's'): Commitment, Control, Challenge.
unverified.discrepancy: Correctly includes the three C's but adds two invented factors (Recovery, Support) that are not part of the hardiness construct.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove Recovery and Support dimensions; official hardiness = 3 factors (Commitment, Control, Challenge).

unverified.framework: Life Orientation Test-Revised (LOT-R), Scheier & Carver — dispositional optimism
unverified.official_structure: Dispositional optimism, essentially unidimensional; at most 2 correlated dimensions (Optimism vs Pessimism), with pessimism items reverse-scored. No 'hopeful/realistic/guarded/proactive/meaning' subscales exist.
unverified.discrepancy: Invents 5 factors (Hopeful, Realistic, Guarded, Proactive, Meaning) that have no basis in the LOT/LOT-R. The real instrument is optimism-vs-pessimism. Also every item is reverse:false, whereas LOT-R defines itself by reverse-keyed pessimism items.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to LOT-R shape: 2 dims 'Optimism' and 'Pessimism' (or a single optimism scale), and add reverse-keyed pessimism items. Drop the invented Realistic/Guarded/Proactive/Meaning dimensions.

unverified.framework: Rumination-Reflection Questionnaire (RRQ), Trapnell & Campbell 1999
unverified.official_structure: Exactly 2 orthogonal factors: Rumination and Reflection (12 items each).
unverified.discrepancy: Keeps the correct 2 core factors but adds 3 invented ones (Problem Solving, Emotional Replay, Release) not in the RRQ, turning a clean 2-factor instrument into a 5-factor one.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reduce to the 2 RRQ factors: 'Rumination' and 'Reflection'. Remove Problem Solving, Emotional Replay, and Release (fold the emotional items into Rumination).

unverified.framework: Loneliness measures — UCLA Loneliness Scale (unidimensional) / Weiss typology (social vs emotional)
unverified.official_structure: UCLA Loneliness Scale is unidimensional; Weiss's recognized typology has 2 independent types: Social Loneliness and Emotional Loneliness. No validated 'existential/transition/protective' loneliness factors.
unverified.discrepancy: Social and Emotional match Weiss, but Existential, Transition, and Protective are invented factors with no authoritative basis, presented as a Tier-A structure.
unverified.proposed_fix: Restrict to Weiss's 2 types (Social, Emotional), or use a single UCLA-style unidimensional scale. Remove Existential/Transition/Protective.

unverified.framework: Self-Determination Theory basic psychological needs (Deci & Ryan)
unverified.official_structure: Exactly 3 basic psychological needs: Autonomy, Competence, Relatedness.
unverified.discrepancy: Correctly includes the 3 real needs but adds 2 invented ones (Meaning, Restoration) that are not SDT basic needs, presenting a 5-need structure.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove 'Meaning' and 'Restoration'; SDT basic needs = 3 (Autonomy, Competence, Relatedness).

unverified.framework: Sensory Processing Sensitivity / Highly Sensitive Person Scale (Aron), DOES model
unverified.official_structure: Aron's DOES model = 4 components: Depth of processing, Overstimulation, Emotional reactivity/empathy, Sensing the Subtle. Empirical HSPS = 1, 2, or 3 factors (AES, EOE, LST). No 'Recovery' factor in either.
unverified.discrepancy: Depth/Overstimulation/Emotional/Subtlety map cleanly to the 4 DOES components, but a 5th factor 'Recovery' is invented and belongs to neither DOES nor the empirical HSPS factor solutions.
unverified.proposed_fix: Drop 'Recovery' (fold its items into Overstimulation) to match the 4-component DOES model.

unverified.framework: Snyder Adult Hope Scale
unverified.official_structure: The Hope Scale is defined by exactly 2 components: Agency (goal-directed determination) and Pathways (perceived routes to goals).
unverified.discrepancy: The framework's defining shape is 2 factors, but the test adds 3 invented dimensions (Optimism, Support, Renewal), distorting the recognizable Agency/Pathways shape even under Tier-B leniency.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reduce to the 2 Snyder components: 'Agency' and 'Pathways'. Remove Optimism, Support, and Renewal.

unverified.framework: Self-Handicapping Scale (SHS), Jones & Rhodewalt 1982
unverified.official_structure: Essentially unidimensional self-esteem-protection scale; where factored, 2 factors are found — behavioral self-handicapping and claimed self-handicapping (excuse-making).
unverified.discrepancy: Invents 5 sub-scales (procrastination, overcommitment, perfectionism, excuse, ownership) that do not correspond to the SHS factor structure — a unidimensional/2-factor scale is wrongly split into 5, and 'ownership' is the opposite (low-handicapping) construct rather than a factor.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to the SHS structure: either a single self-handicapping scale, or 2 factors (behavioral self-handicapping, claimed self-handicapping); reverse-key the 'ownership' items into the main scale rather than treating them as a standalone factor.

unverified.framework: Person-Organization Fit / value congruence (Cable & DeRue perceived P-O fit; O'Reilly, Chatman & Caldwell OCP)
unverified.official_structure: No single authoritative factor structure. Perceived P-O fit is often unidimensional (value congruence, Cable & DeRue 2002); the OCP operationalizes culture via ~7-8 value dimensions that vary by study. There is no canonical 6-factor P-O fit model.
unverified.discrepancy: The 6 dimensions (mission, autonomy, structure, innovation, community, reward) are a reasonable work-values set but do not match any published P-O fit factor structure; no authoritative fixed structure exists to validate against.

unverified.framework: Change Style Indicator (CSI), Discovery Learning / W.C. Musselwhite
unverified.official_structure: 3 styles on a single continuum: Conserver — Pragmatist — Originator.
unverified.discrepancy: The CSI is known for exactly 3 styles; this test adds two invented dimensions (Stabilizer, Translator), inflating the framework's shape to 5.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove 'stabilizer' and 'translator'; keep only the 3 canonical styles Conserver, Pragmatist, Originator (a continuum).

unverified.framework: Communication Accommodation Theory (CAT), Howard Giles
unverified.official_structure: Core accommodation strategies: convergence, divergence, and maintenance (with over-accommodation and under-accommodation as recognized forms).
unverified.discrepancy: The three core strategies are present and correctly named, but the test adds 'code-switching' and 'bridging', which are not CAT accommodation strategies, inflating the framework's shape.
unverified.proposed_fix: Drop 'codeswitch' and 'bridging' (or replace with the recognized over-accommodation / under-accommodation), keeping the three canonical CAT strategies.

unverified.framework: Enneagram instinctual variants / subtypes
unverified.official_structure: 3 instinctual variants only: Self-Preservation, Social, Sexual (One-to-One). Confirmed by Wikipedia and The Enneagram Institute.
unverified.discrepancy: Adds an invented 4th dimension 'Balanced' that is not a recognized instinctual variant; the framework has exactly 3 instincts.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'balanced' dimension and its 2 questions; keep only the 3 instinctual variants (self-preservation, social, sexual).

unverified.framework: Enneagram Harmonic groups (Riso-Hudson)
unverified.official_structure: 3 Harmonic groups: Positive Outlook (2,7,9), Competency (1,3,5), Reactive/Emotional Realness (4,6,8).
unverified.discrepancy: Adds an invented 4th dimension 'Adaptive'; the Harmonic grouping has exactly 3 groups.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'adaptive' dimension and its 2 questions; keep only the 3 harmonic groups (positive, competency, reactive).

unverified.framework: Enneagram Hornevian groups (from Karen Horney's 3 trends)
unverified.official_structure: 3 Hornevian groups mapping to Horney's three trends: Assertive (moving against, 3/7/8), Compliant/Dutiful (moving toward, 1/2/6), Withdrawn (moving away, 4/5/9).
unverified.discrepancy: Adds an invented 4th dimension 'Flexible'; the Hornevian grouping has exactly 3 groups (Horney defined 3 trends).
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'flexible' dimension and its 2 questions; keep only the 3 Hornevian groups (assertive, compliant, withdrawn).

unverified.framework: Truity TypeFinder (MBTI-style)
unverified.official_structure: 4 dichotomies only: E-I, S-N, T-F, J-P (plus sub-facets, no 5th axis)
unverified.discrepancy: Adds an invented 5th dimension 'Confidence'; TypeFinder has no confidence/identity/assertiveness axis (that is a 16Personalities Assertive-Turbulent concept, not TypeFinder).
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'confidence' dimension and its 2 items; TypeFinder = 4 dichotomies (energy/information/decision/structure).

unverified.framework: Socionics (information elements / functions)
unverified.official_structure: Socionics functions = Logic (T), Ethics (F), Intuition (N), Sensing (S) — 4 information aspects; typing also uses the 4 Jungian dichotomies. 'Valuing' is not a function or dichotomy. (Socionics is not a validated scientific instrument — arguably belongs in a lower tier.)
unverified.discrepancy: First 4 dims (Logic/Ethics/Intuition/Sensing) are the real socionics functions, but 'Valuing' is an invented 5th factor with no basis in socionics structure.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'valuing' dimension and its 2 items; keep the 4 canonical functions (logic/ethics/intuition/sensing).

unverified.framework: Adult romantic attachment (Bartholomew four-category model)
unverified.official_structure: 4 attachment styles: Secure, Anxious-preoccupied, Dismissive-avoidant, Fearful-avoidant (ECR uses 2 dimensions: anxiety & avoidance). 'Earned secure' is a developmental concept, not a measured attachment category.
unverified.discrepancy: Core 4 styles (secure/anxious/avoidant/fearful) are correct, but adds an invented 5th dimension 'Earned Secure' that is not part of any standard attachment-measurement model.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'earned' dimension and its 2 items; keep the 4-category model (secure/anxious/avoidant/fearful).

unverified.framework: Gallup CliftonStrengths / StrengthsFinder domain model
unverified.official_structure: 4 domains: Executing, Influencing, Relationship Building, Strategic Thinking (grouping the 34 themes)
unverified.discrepancy: Test defines 5 domains and invents a 'Learning' domain that does not exist in the CliftonStrengths model; also renames 'Relationship Building' to 'Relating' and 'Strategic Thinking' to 'Strategic'. The official framework has exactly 4 domains.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the invented 'learning' dimension (Learning is a theme, not a domain) and reduce to the 4 official domains; rename 'strategic' -> 'Strategic Thinking' and 'relating' -> 'Relationship Building'. Redistribute the two 'learning' items into Strategic Thinking.

unverified.framework: PrinciplesYou (Ray Dalio / Adam Grant) personality archetypes
unverified.official_structure: PrinciplesYou reports ~28 named archetypes derived from underlying trait scores, not a small set of top-level 'dimensions' like Independent/Creator/Advisor/Operator/Connector.
unverified.discrepancy: The 5 dimension labels are invented groupings that do not correspond to PrinciplesYou's published archetype set; could not confirm any authoritative 5-factor public structure (site cookie-gated). As Tier C entertainment the framing is loose, so no clean pop-framework structure to check against.

unverified.framework: Generic 'work style' typology (no single authoritative scientific instrument)
unverified.official_structure: No published scientific instrument named 'Workstyle' with the factors Planner/Driver/Analyst/Collaborator/Supporter exists; these resemble ad-hoc social-style/DISC-adjacent labels.
unverified.discrepancy: Labeled Tier A (public scientific instrument) but there is no authoritative public factor structure to validate against; the 5 dimensions appear invented.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify as Tier C (entertainment) since no validated instrument backs it, or retitle to reference a specific real model (e.g. Merrill-Reid Social Styles = 4 styles: Driver, Expressive, Amiable, Analytical) and align dimensions accordingly.

unverified.framework: Cattell 16PF Questionnaire
unverified.official_structure: 16 primary factors (A Warmth, B Reasoning, C Emotional Stability, E Dominance, F Liveliness, G Rule-Consciousness, H Social Boldness, I Sensitivity, L Vigilance, M Abstractedness, N Privateness, O Apprehension, Q1 Openness to Change, Q2 Self-Reliance, Q3 Perfectionism, Q4 Tension)
unverified.discrepancy: Only 8 of the 16 defining primary factors are represented (the first 8, A-I). The framework's identity is its 16 factors (the number is in its name); half are missing, so the shape is wrong.
unverified.proposed_fix: Add the missing 8 primary factors (Vigilance, Abstractedness, Privateness, Apprehension, Openness to Change, Self-Reliance, Perfectionism, Tension) so all 16 are covered, or explicitly reframe as an 8-trait subset; a 16PF-style test should reflect 16 factors.

unverified.framework: Pop relationship 'red flag / green flag' concept (no validated instrument)
unverified.official_structure: None. 'Red flag / green flag' is internet/pop-culture relationship shorthand with no published factor structure or validated psychometric instrument.
unverified.discrepancy: Mis-tiered as A (public scientific instrument). No authoritative scientific structure exists to validate its 5 invented dimensions against.
unverified.proposed_fix: Retier to C (entertainment/wellbeing) and ensure 'for reflection, not diagnostic' framing; the 5 dimensions are a reasonable self-authored set but must not imply scientific validation.

unverified.framework: 'Toxic traits' self-reflection (no validated instrument)
unverified.official_structure: None. No standard validated 'toxic traits' instrument; dimensions loosely echo antagonism/Dark-Triad facets but map to no single authoritative published structure.
unverified.discrepancy: Mis-tiered as A. The 5 dimensions (entitlement, manipulation, hostility, irresponsibility, accountability) are self-authored with no authoritative factor structure to check against.
unverified.proposed_fix: Retier to C/wellbeing with clearly non-diagnostic framing; or, if science is intended, rebuild on a validated model (e.g. HEXACO Honesty-Humility or SD3 Dark Triad) with matching dimensions.

unverified.framework: Lise Bourbeau's Five Wounds (self-help), not a validated instrument
unverified.official_structure: Lise Bourbeau's 5 wounds: Rejection, Abandonment, Humiliation, Betrayal, Injustice — a pop/self-help model, not a peer-validated psychometric scale.
unverified.discrepancy: The 5 dimensions exactly match Bourbeau's five-wounds model (structurally faithful), but it is mis-tiered as A: Bourbeau's model is self-help, with no validated scientific factor structure to verify against.
unverified.proposed_fix: Retier to C (pop/self-help) with reflective, non-diagnostic framing; the 5-wound structure is faithful to Bourbeau and needs no dimensional change.

unverified.framework: Self-adaptation of Chapman's 5 Love Languages
unverified.official_structure: Chapman's 5 Love Languages (Words of Affirmation, Quality Time, Acts of Service, Receiving Gifts, Physical Touch) is a proprietary pop framework with no validated scientific factor structure; 'self-love languages' is an unofficial derivative. No authoritative published factor structure exists.
unverified.discrepancy: Mislabeled Tier A: no peer-validated instrument backs a 'self-love languages' factor structure. The 5 Chapman languages map cleanly (words/time/acts/gifts/body=Physical Touch); a 6th 'boundaries' scale is an added self-care extension beyond Chapman's five.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier B/C (pop adaptation). Structure itself is acceptable as an inspired-by extension; no scientific factor claim should be implied.

unverified.framework: Generic dating-values quiz (no established instrument)
unverified.official_structure: No published psychometric instrument named or structured as a 'dating standards test' exists. The 6 dimensions (values/chemistry/lifestyle/safety/growth/freedom) are a bespoke content taxonomy, not a validated factor structure.
unverified.discrepancy: Mislabeled Tier A: there is no authoritative public factor structure to match against. Content is reasonable but not a scientific instrument.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reclassify to Tier C (values/preferences quiz); remove any implied scientific validity.

unverified.framework: Open Extended Jungian Type Scales (OEJTS), public-domain, openpsychometrics.org
unverified.official_structure: OEJTS measures EXACTLY 4 scales, one per MBTI dichotomy: I/E, S/N, F/T, J/P (48 items, 12 per scale).
unverified.discrepancy: A 5th dimension 'confidence' (labeled 'Clarity') is invented; OEJTS has no such scale. Its items measure self-rated stability of type, not a Jungian dichotomy.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'confidence'/'Clarity' dimension and its 2 items; OEJTS has exactly 4 scales (E/I, N/S, T/F, J/P).

unverified.framework: Short Dark Triad (SD3), Jones & Paulhus (2014)
unverified.official_structure: SD3 has EXACTLY 3 subscales: Narcissism, Machiavellianism, Psychopathy (27 items, 9 per subscale).
unverified.discrepancy: A 4th dimension 'accountability' is invented; the SD3 has no accountability subscale. Its items measure prosocial repair, orthogonal to the dark triad.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'accountability' dimension and its 2 items; SD3 = 3 factors (Narcissism, Machiavellianism, Psychopathy).

unverified.framework: Protestant Work Ethic Scale (Mirels & Garrett 1971) / Multidimensional Work Ethic Profile (Miller et al. 2002)
unverified.official_structure: The classic Protestant Ethic Scale (Mirels & Garrett) is scored as a single unidimensional construct; the established multidimensional instrument (MWEP) has 7 factors: self-reliance, morality/ethics, leisure, hard work/centrality of work, wasted time, delay of gratification, and centrality of work.
unverified.discrepancy: Uses 5 dimensions (diligence, discipline, frugality, calling, leisure) that match neither the unidimensional Mirels-Garrett scale nor the 7-factor MWEP. 'Frugality' and 'calling' are not MWEP subscales; MWEP's self-reliance, morality, centrality, and wasted-time factors are absent. The facets are Weberian-construct-plausible but do not reproduce any published factor structure.
unverified.proposed_fix: Align to MWEP's 7 factors (self-reliance, morality/ethics, leisure, hard work, centrality of work, wasted time, delay of gratification) OR retitle as a work-ethic construct scale; current frugality/calling dims have no scale basis.

unverified.framework: Nature Relatedness Scale (Nisbet, Zelenski & Murphy 2009)
unverified.official_structure: 3 subscales: NR-Self (identity/connection), NR-Perspective (environmental concern/worldview), NR-Experience (physical familiarity and desire for contact with nature).
unverified.discrepancy: Uses 5 dimensions (identity, emotion, experience, care, restoration). 'Emotion' and 'restoration' are invented and not NR factors (restoration belongs to Attention Restoration Theory, a different framework); the real 3-factor structure is over-split.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to the 3 published factors: identity->NR-Self, care->NR-Perspective, experience->NR-Experience; drop or fold 'emotion' and 'restoration'.

unverified.framework: Nonverbal Immediacy Scale (Richmond, McCroskey & Johnson 2003)
unverified.official_structure: The NIS (26-item self- and other-report) is scored as a single overall nonverbal-immediacy composite; it is treated as unidimensional with no validated behavioral subscales.
unverified.discrepancy: Splits a unidimensional scale into 5 invented facets (eye, posture, expression, proximity, attunement). 'Attunement' (adjusting to cultural/personal nonverbal differences) is not part of the immediacy construct at all.
unverified.proposed_fix: Score as one immediacy dimension; remove the invented eye/posture/expression/proximity/attunement subscale split (drop attunement items entirely).

unverified.framework: Inventory of Interpersonal Problems - Circumplex (IIP-C, Alden/Wiggins/Pincus)
unverified.official_structure: The interpersonal circumplex is partitioned into 8 octants: PA Domineering, BC Vindictive, DE Cold, FG Socially Inhibited, HI Nonassertive, JK Overly Accommodating, LM Self-Sacrificing, NO Intrusive/Needy.
unverified.discrepancy: Provides only 6 octants (dominant, submissive, distant, needy, giving, hostile). A circumplex by definition requires all 8 octants; FG Socially Inhibited and JK Overly Accommodating are missing, breaking the circular structure the title claims.
unverified.proposed_fix: Add the two missing octants -- 'socially-inhibited' (FG) and 'overly-accommodating' (JK) -- for the full 8-octant circumplex, each with balanced items.

unverified.framework: Big Five Aspects Scale (DeYoung, Quilty & Peterson 2007)
unverified.official_structure: The BFAS defines 10 aspects, two per domain: Openness/Intellect, Industriousness/Orderliness (Conscientiousness), Enthusiasm/Assertiveness (Extraversion), Compassion/Politeness (Agreeableness), Volatility/Withdrawal (Neuroticism).
unverified.discrepancy: Uses only the 5 Big Five domains, not the 10 aspects. The defining feature of the 'Aspects Scale' -- splitting each domain into two aspects -- is entirely absent; it is a plain Big Five test mislabeled as the Aspects Scale (item wording even hints at both aspects but they aren't separately scored).
unverified.proposed_fix: Split each domain into its two aspects for 10 dimensions (Intellect/Openness, Industriousness/Orderliness, Enthusiasm/Assertiveness, Compassion/Politeness, Volatility/Withdrawal), 1+ item each.

unverified.framework: Adult (Trait) Hope Scale (Snyder et al. 1991)
unverified.official_structure: 2 subscales: Agency thinking (goal-directed determination) and Pathways thinking (ability to generate routes to goals); 4 items each plus 4 fillers.
unverified.discrepancy: Uses 5 dimensions (agency, pathways, persistence, support, future). 'Persistence', 'support', and 'future' are invented; the scale has only Agency and Pathways.
unverified.proposed_fix: Reduce to the 2 published factors (agency, pathways); fold persistence/future items into agency and drop 'support' (not part of the construct).

unverified.framework: Life Orientation Test-Revised (Scheier, Carver & Bridges 1994)
unverified.official_structure: Measures dispositional optimism; 6 scored items yielding two correlated dimensions, Optimism and Pessimism (often reported as a single optimism score).
unverified.discrepancy: Uses 4 dimensions (optimism, pessimism, realism, coping). 'Realism' and 'coping' are invented factors with no basis in the LOT-R.
unverified.proposed_fix: Keep only Optimism and Pessimism dimensions; remove the invented 'realism' and 'coping' dims and their items.

unverified.framework: Satisfaction With Life Scale (SWLS; Diener, Emmons, Larsen & Griffin, 1985)
unverified.official_structure: Unidimensional: a single global life-satisfaction factor (5 items, no subscales). It deliberately does NOT decompose into life domains.
unverified.discrepancy: Real SWLS is a single global construct with no facets; this invents 5 sub-dimensions (fulfillment, alignment, gratitude, agency, future) that do not exist in the instrument. (Also mis-tiered: SWLS is a free public scientific scale, i.e. Tier A.)
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to a single 'life satisfaction' dimension; SWLS is unidimensional and assesses global satisfaction, not sub-facets.

unverified.framework: Self-Report Altruism Scale (SRA; Rushton, Chrisjohn & Fekken, 1981)
unverified.official_structure: Unidimensional: 20 items on a single altruism factor, scored as one total (frequency of specific altruistic behaviors).
unverified.discrepancy: The unidimensional SRA is wrongly split into 5 invented factors (generosity, spontaneous, costly, civic, quiet). Items are also reframed as traits/attitudes rather than the SRA's behavior-frequency format.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to a single 'altruism' dimension; SRA has no published subscales.

unverified.framework: Brief Self-Control Scale (BSCS; Tangney, Baumeister & Boone, 2004)
unverified.official_structure: Scored as a single total self-control score (13 items). The most-cited alternative is a 2-factor solution (restraint / impulsivity); never 5 facets.
unverified.discrepancy: BSCS is scored unidimensionally (at most 2 factors in the literature) but is split into 5 invented facets (impulse, habit, attention, emotion, recovery), none of which are published subscales.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to a single total self-control dimension (or at most the 2 documented factors); remove the invented facets.

unverified.framework: Need to Belong Scale (NTBS; Leary, Kelly, Cottrell & Schreindorfer, 2013)
unverified.official_structure: Unidimensional: 10 items on a single need-to-belong factor (3 items reverse-keyed).
unverified.discrepancy: The single-factor NTBS is split into 5 invented dimensions (acceptance, closeness, group, rejection, independence). The 'independence' items are the scale's reverse-keyed content promoted to a positive standalone factor and keyed reverse=false, distorting the total.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to a single 'need to belong' dimension; reverse-key the low-belonging ('independence') items rather than treating them as a separate positive factor.

unverified.framework: Authenticity Scale (Wood, Linley, Maltby, Baliousis & Joseph, 2008)
unverified.official_structure: Three subscales: Authentic Living, Accepting External Influence, and Self-Alienation (12 items).
unverified.discrepancy: Correctly captures the 3 real subscales (living=Authentic Living, influence=Accepting External Influence, alienation=Self-Alienation) but adds 2 invented factors (expression, reflection) not in the instrument, so the shape is wrong (5 vs 3).
unverified.proposed_fix: Drop the invented 'expression' and 'reflection' dimensions; the Authenticity Scale is a 3-factor model.

unverified.framework: Mindful Attention Awareness Scale (MAAS; Brown & Ryan, 2003)
unverified.official_structure: Unidimensional: 15 items loading on a single factor (present-moment attention/awareness); reverse-scored and averaged to one score.
unverified.discrepancy: The explicitly single-factor MAAS is split into 5 invented dimensions (present, autopilot, body, emotion, return), none of which are published subscales. MAAS items are also normally negatively worded (mindlessness) and reverse-scored; here all are positively keyed.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to a single 'mindful attention/awareness' dimension; MAAS is unidimensional.

unverified.framework: Self-Consciousness Scale (Fenigstein, Scheier & Buss, 1975; SCS-R Scheier & Carver, 1985)
unverified.official_structure: Three factors: Private Self-Consciousness, Public Self-Consciousness, and Social Anxiety.
unverified.discrepancy: Correctly captures the 3 real factors (private, public, anxiety=Social Anxiety) but adds 2 invented factors (reflection, presentation), giving 5 vs the published 3.
unverified.proposed_fix: Drop the invented 'reflection' and 'presentation' dimensions; SCS is a 3-factor model (Private SC, Public SC, Social Anxiety).

unverified.framework: Social Connectedness Scale (Lee & Robbins, 1995; SCS-R Lee, Draper & Lee, 2001)
unverified.official_structure: Unidimensional: a single social-connectedness factor (original 8 items; revised SCS-R 20 items, still one factor).
unverified.discrepancy: The single-factor Social Connectedness Scale is split into 5 invented dimensions (belonging, closeness, inclusion, continuity, ease), none of which are published subscales.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse to a single 'social connectedness' dimension; the scale is unidimensional.

unverified.framework: Eysenck Personality Questionnaire-Revised (EPQ-R)
unverified.official_structure: 4 scales: Extraversion (E), Neuroticism (N), Psychoticism (P), and Lie/social-desirability (L) validity scale. Eysenck's model = 3 PEN dimensions + a Lie scale.
unverified.discrepancy: Adds an invented 5th scale 'restraint'. The real EPQ-R has only E, N, P, and the Lie scale — there is no restraint/impulse-control factor in Eysenck's structure.
unverified.proposed_fix: Remove the 'restraint' dimension and its 2 items; keep only extraversion, neuroticism, psychoticism, and desirability (Lie scale). EPQ-R = 4 scales.

unverified.framework: Bernreuter Personality Inventory
unverified.official_structure: 6 scales: Neurotic tendency, Self-sufficiency, Introversion-extraversion, Dominance-submission, Sociability, Confidence
unverified.discrepancy: Provides 5 dims (sociability, dominance, confidence, stability=neurotic tendency, self-sufficiency) but omits the core Introversion-extraversion scale, one of the four original Bernreuter scales.
unverified.proposed_fix: Add an 'introversion-extraversion' dimension (2 balanced items) to cover all 6 Bernreuter scales.

unverified.framework: Generic sentence-completion / incomplete-sentences projective method
unverified.official_structure: No single authoritative dimension structure — sentence-completion is a projective format used across many differently-scored instruments (e.g. WUSCT scores ego development; RISB scores overall adjustment). Framing is honest ('-style'), dims plausible.

unverified.framework: Generic projective-technique personality assessment
unverified.official_structure: No single authoritative framework — 'projective personality' is an umbrella category, not one instrument with a defined factor structure. Dims (structure, story, emotion, symbol, defense) are plausible; framing is honest.

unverified.framework: Q-sort methodology (California Q-Set / Block Q-sort)
unverified.official_structure: Q-sort is an assessment METHOD (rank-ordering descriptors along a forced distribution), not a scale with a published factor structure. There is no authoritative set of factors named 'central/aspirational/rejected/contextual/integrated'.
unverified.discrepancy: The five dimensions are invented; Q-sort has no canonical factor structure to match against, so fidelity cannot be scored against a published model.

unverified.framework: Multidimensional Introversion-Extraversion Scales (MIES), Open-Source Psychometrics Project
unverified.official_structure: The MIES source pages confirm E-I is broken into several related sub-traits, but the development document and test pages do not publish the final named scale set in retrievable form; no authoritative factor list could be confirmed.
unverified.discrepancy: Cannot confirm the test's five dims (social/thinking/activity/excitement/recovery) match MIES's actual published subscales; the instrument's authoritative structure was not retrievable to verify count or names.

unverified.framework: Rosenberg Self-Esteem Scale (RSES)
unverified.official_structure: Unidimensional: a single global self-esteem score. 10 items (5 positively worded, 5 negatively worded/reverse-scored), scored 0-30.
unverified.discrepancy: The RSES is a unidimensional global-self-esteem scale, but the test wrongly splits it into 5 invented factors (respect/worth/competence/acceptance/criticism). Also, all items are keyed reverse:false, whereas the RSES uses 5 reverse-scored items.
unverified.proposed_fix: Collapse the 5 sub-factors into one 'self-esteem' dimension (RSES is unidimensional). Keep ~5 positively worded and ~5 negatively worded items, and set reverse:true on the negatively worded items (the current 'criticism' items).

unverified.framework: Survey of Dictionary-Based Isms (SDI), Saucier — lexical structure of social attitudes/beliefs
unverified.official_structure: Saucier's SDI captures a basic FOUR dimensions plus a fifth (SDI-46): empirically derived isms factors (Tradition-Oriented Religiousness/Traditionalism, Subjective Spirituality, Unmitigated Self-Interest, Communal Rationalism, plus a fifth). Not generic bipolar value pairs.
unverified.discrepancy: The test uses 6 invented bipolar value poles (tradition/progress, individualism/collectivism, skepticism/idealism) that do not correspond to Saucier's empirically derived isms factors; both the count (6 vs 4-5) and the factor names are wrong.
unverified.proposed_fix: Replace the 6 bipolar dims with Saucier's SDI factors: Traditionalism/Tradition-Oriented Religiousness, Subjective Spirituality, Unmitigated Self-Interest, Communal Rationalism (+ optional 5th), with items keyed to each factor rather than opposing value pairs.

unverified.framework: Forté Communication Style Profile (DISC-lineage proprietary instrument)
unverified.official_structure: 4 core traits: Dominance, Extroversion, Patience, Conformity (Adaptability is a separate derived report concept, not a 5th co-equal core trait)
